title: E5 Enclave FarmBlock Site Selection Baseline Dataset
description: Community-level food security, environmental health, demographic, and historical context data for proposed FarmBlock research deployment sites: Liberty City, Miami FL and Pine Bluff, AR.

demographics.source: FIU Community Background Report 2024 / US Census

environmental_health.black_american_pollution_risk_multiplier: 75% more likely to live near industrial pollution sources vs white Americans
environmental_health.source: EPA EJScreen / NAACP Environmental Justice Report

historical_context.notes: Historic Black neighborhood in Miami-Dade. Site of 1980 McDuffie riots — catalyzed by police brutality against Black residents. Deep history of systemic disinvestment, redlining, and urban displacement.
historical_context.strategic_rationale: Highest-density BIPOC food apartheid zone in South Florida. Proximity to Black community organizations, churches, and reentry programs operated by BDI.

research_rationale.ai_deployment_feasibility: High — dense urban grid, existing community relationships
research_rationale.proposed_research_focus: Soil-to-health outcome correlation; urban microbiome diversity vs respiratory disease incidence

demographics.source: US Census Bureau QuickFacts 2024 / DataUSA / DataCommons 2025

food_access.source: USDA ERS Food Security Report 2024 / Arkansas Governor Food Desert Working Group 2022

historical_context.notes: Pine Bluff was a major Freedmen's Bureau site post-Civil War. Jefferson County has one of the highest concentrations of Black descendants of enslaved people in Arkansas. City has experienced severe population decline, disinvestment, and industrial abandonment since 1980s.
historical_context.abandoned_properties: Significant — low-cost land availability creates agricultural deployment opportunity
historical_context.strategic_rationale: Freedmen legacy community with acute food apartheid, 74% Black population, 24% poverty rate, distressed property market enabling low-cost site acquisition.

hbcu_proximity.institution: University of Arkansas at Pine Bluff (UAPB)
hbcu_proximity.notes: UAPB's agriculture program includes urban farming research. 1890 Land Grant mandate explicitly covers food security and rural/urban agriculture for underserved communities.

research_rationale.food_apartheid_severity: Critical — state ranked most food insecure in US
research_rationale.ai_deployment_feasibility: High — low land cost, UAPB proximity, established Black community networks
research_rationale.proposed_research_focus: Agricultural yield modeling; food security outcome measurement; soil quality baseline in post-industrial zones

national_context.bipoc_overrepresentation: BIPOC Americans disproportionately concentrated in USDA-designated food desert census tracts
national_context.black_pollution_risk: Black Americans 75% more likely to live near industrial pollution sources vs white Americans
sources: USDA ERS Food Security Key Statistics 2024
NAACP Environmental and Climate Justice Report
Arkansas Governor Food Desert Working Group Report 2022

data_sources_cited: USDA Economic Research Service — Food Access Research Atlas (ers.usda.gov)
US Census Bureau QuickFacts — Pine Bluff AR and Miami FL
Florida International University Community Background Report — Liberty City 2024
Arkansas Center for Health Improvement — Food Deserts in Arkansas 2022
Arkansas Governor's Food Desert Working Group Report December 2022
USDA ERS Food Security in the US Key Statistics 2024
EPA EJScreen Environmental Justice Mapping Tool
intended_use: Baseline site selection dataset for FarmBlock urban agriculture and environmental health research program. To be used as foundation for IoT sensor deployment design, research hypothesis development, and community outreach planning.
